LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS STOW LAKE CORPORATION AND SURREY CYCLE RENTALS, INC. DBA WHEEL FUN RENTALS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND/OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE. - ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PETITIONERS' REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION/ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THE RFQ PROCESS WAS CONDUCTED IMPARTIALLY AND WITH FAIRNESS TO ALL BIDDERS. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF FAVORITISM, FRAUD OR CORRUPTION. THE FACT THAT RESPONDENT REFERRED REAL PARTY IN INTEREST TO A LOBBYIST TO ASSIST WITH PR BEFORE THE AWARD ISSUE WAS PRESENTED TO THE COMMISSION AND BOARD OF SUPERVISORS BUT AFTER THE AWARD, IS NOT EVIDENCE OF ANY IMPROPRIETY INVOLVING THE EVALUATION PROCESS. IN FACT, PETITIONER ALSO EMPLOYED LOBBYIST IN PRIOR BID PROCESSES AND DURING THIS PROCESS AS WELL. MOREOVER, THE OTHER ALLEGED IRREGULARITIES PLAINTIFF COMPLAINS OF DO NOT RENDER THE RFQ PROCESS IMPROPER OR INVALID. FINALLY, THE BALANCE OF THE HARM TO PETITIONERS VS. RESPONDENT AND REAL PARTY IS OF EQUAL WEIGHT. MOREOVER, ANY HARM PETITIONER MAY SUFFER CAN BE MONETARILY COMPENSATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. IF PARTIES CAN NOT AGREE ON THE FORM OF ORDER, EACH SIDE MAY PRESENT THEIR OWN BY TOMORROW MORNING (05/18/11) FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction/ Alternative Writ Of Mandate (Calendared In Dept. 301 In Error)
Off Calendar May-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONERS STOW LAKE CORPORATION AND SURREY CYCLE RENTALS, INC. DBA WHEEL FUN RENTALS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND/OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE.

APPEARANCES: Paul S. Rosenlund, Esq. 957-3178 & Oliver L. Holmes, Esq. 957-3025 of Duane Morris LLP for petitioners & moving parties; Donald P. Margolis, 554-3853 & Francesca Gessner, 554-4762 Deputy City Attys for respondents & opposing parties City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Board of Supervisors, San Francisco Recreation and Park Commission, San Francisco Recreation and Park Department; Neil H. O'Donnell, Esq. & Thomas D. Blanford, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell 926-2828 for Real Parties in Interest & opposing parties Stow Lake Boathouse Company, LLC, Cloudless Skies Park Company, LLC, Armand Ortega.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PETITIONERS' REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION/ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THE RFQ PROCESS WAS CONDUCTED IMPARTIALLY AND WITH FAIRNESS TO ALL BIDDERS. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF FAVORITISM, FRAUD OR CORRUPTION. THE FACT THAT RESPONDENT REFERRED REAL PARTY IN INTEREST TO A LOBBYIST TO ASSIST WITH PR BEFORE THE AWARD ISSUE WAS PRESENTED TO THE COMMISSION AND BOARD OF SUPERVISORS BUT AFTER THE AWARD, IS NOT EVIDENCE OF ANY IMPROPRIETY INVOLVING THE EVALUATION PROCESS. IN FACT, PETITIONER ALSO EMPLOYED LOBBYIST IN PRIOR BID PROCESSES AND DURING THIS PROCESS AS WELL. MOREOVER, THE OTHER ALLEGED IRREGULARITIES PLAINTIFF COMPLAINS OF DO NOT RENDER THE RFQ PROCESS IMPROPER OR INVALID. FINALLY, THE BALANCE OF THE HARM TO PETITIONERS VS. RESPONDENT AND REAL PARTY IS OF EQUAL WEIGHT. MOREOVER, ANY HARM PETITIONER MAY SUFFER CAN BE MONETARILY COMPENSATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. If parties can not agree on the form of order, each side may present their own by tomorrow morning (05/18/11) for the Court to determine. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
